data.content: The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file `obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`, but it doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk, but the compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` items using an `ItemGroup` inside the target, after writing the file. Something like:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target after the `WriteCodeFile` task call.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation. This works because MSBuild allows dynamic item additions inside targets that run `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"`.
